title: Coverage, Thresholds, and Meaningful Evidence · Gate Defense
summary: Set coverage policy that exposes risk without encouraging empty tests. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a changed-code coverage and exception policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret line, branch, function, condition, and path coverage limitations.
Choose global, package, and changed-code thresholds from risk and baseline.
Exclude generated or unreachable code with reviewed narrow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision

Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Coverage Review through the concrete a changed-code coverage and exception policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.
